input.headline: Wicked: For Good saved by the magnetic performances of its stars

raw.headline: Wicked: For Good saved by the magnetic performances of its stars

winner: The cast of *Wicked* performed so magnetically that one audience member was last seen crawling toward the stage, metal spoon in hand, trying to scrape off his new nickname.

candidates.text: Elphaba and Glinda’s acting was so good, I half-expected them to levitate—but no, just my expectations floating away after that show.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Witty wordplay with 'levitate' and 'expectations floating away,' specific to the show's context, concise, and surprising."}

candidates.text: Why did the wicked witch need a magnet? Because her charm was so strong, she could attract any performance!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat clever with the wordplay on 'magnet' and 'charm,' but it's a bit forced and lacks specificity to the headline. The reference to 'Wicked: For Good' isn't clearly tied to the magnetic performances mentioned."}

candidates.text: The cast of *Wicked* performed so magnetically that one audience member was last seen crawling toward the stage, metal spoon in hand, trying to scrape off his new nickname.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising and specific twist with the audience member and metal spoon, ties back to 'magnetically' in a clever way."}

candidates.text: The cast of "Wicked" was so magnetic, they pulled a whole musical off stage and into the heart!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Creative use of 'magnetic' from the headline, concise and surprising twist with 'pulled a whole musical off stage,' and ties back to the performance quality mentioned."}

candidates.text: The show *Wicked* was so bad, even the witches' brooms were better at flying—turns out, their performances were the only thing holding this "for good" story together.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat surprising and specific to the show, but the humor feels a bit forced with the broom comparison. It's not offensive or generic, but lacks the sharpness needed for a higher score."}
